context: BoardSummaryChips (shared scrape diagnostics UI component) is used by both jobs and autopilot features. A naive placement in features/jobs/ or features/autopilot/ would violate ESLint no-cross-feature-import rule.
decision: Place the component under components/scrape/ (not inside any features/* dir). This mirrors existing cross-feature component homes (components/generation/, components/resume/, components/interview/). The shared domain justifies the shared components/ location, and ESLint treats components/<domain>/ as public API (cross-feature imports allowed there).
outcome: BoardSummaryChips reused by JobsPage + AutopilotCard with zero boundary violations. The no-cross-feature-import rule is satisfied. Future shared UI (e.g., scrape-result chips, provider status) has a natural home.

context: BoardSummaryChips.sanitizeReason (client-side) mirrors Rust redact_token (autopilot_helpers). Both redact URLs, paths, IPv4, emails, credentials. Changes must stay in lockstep or redaction gaps appear (user sees sensitive data the other side didn't redact).
decision: Document the lockstep dependency: add rustdoc comment on redact_token pointing at sanitizeReason and vice-versa. Create a shared fixture table for redaction test cases (UNC paths, IPv6, host:port, email, credential patterns) that both implementations use. Known gap: IPv6 redaction is not implemented on either side yet (marked TODO, deferred as shared follow-up). Input pre-cap (1000 chars) is independent of output cap (200 chars) to bound work on pathological strings.
outcome: Intentional gap (IPv6) is documented and tracked. Lockstep enforcement is visible in code. Test coverage is unified: both sides validate against the same fixtures, preventing accidental divergence.

context: JobsPage listens to global jobs:event channel for job.completed and job.failed. The channel is shared by scrape, AI, autopilot, and agent/pipeline jobs — job.failed from ANY job type fires the handler. Without a jobId check, an unrelated background job failure (e.g., autopilot run) blanks the scrape chips and paints a foreign error as 'Last scrape failed'.
decision: Gate the failed-job handler on `isActiveJob = ev.jobId === scrapeJobRef.current` computed BEFORE the handler clears scrapeJobRef (mirror existing job.completed guard). Both setLastSummaries/setLastFailureNote gated on isActiveJob. Any new UI state updated from job events (chips, status badges, error notes) must guard on active job id — not just scrape-specific code.
outcome: Stale job.failed from other job types does NOT wipe scrape state or paint false errors. The pattern scales to new per-job-type state added to jobs page or other features.

context: Light theme applied utilities.css opacity remaps (e.g., text-foreground/50 → higher opacity for readability). The remap covers primary utilities but misses variants like text-red-400/80 (status colors in light mode). Missed variants render too faint in light theme.
decision: Audit utilities.css remap rules and cross-check against all usage sites (grep for /[0-9][0-9] in classNames). Extend remap to cover status utilities (text-red-400, text-amber-400, text-green-400) and any other high-opacity variant used in light theme. The remap is systemic (one place) but incomplete — capturing all variants requires listing them explicitly.
outcome: Status colors and secondary text read cleanly in both light and dark themes. Audits future light-theme usage, preventing silent faintness.
